keyPoints: M - Management of memory: Allocation, Deallocation, Fragmentation
E - Efficient use of memory: Minimizing overhead
L - Limitations: Fixed, Virtual, and Page-based
O - Optimization: Swapping, Caching, and Prefetching
D - Demand Paging: Reducing memory usage
Y - Yielding control: Allowing other processes to run
P - Paging: Dividing memory into fixed-size blocks
R - Replacement algorithms: LRU, FIFO, and Optimal
E - External fragmentation: Non-contiguous free spaces
N - Non-contiguous allocation: Allocating memory in chunks
S - Swapping: Transferring data between disk and memory
